name: pilot-angular
description: Angular / TypeScript codebase governance: component patterns, signal migration, standalone best practices, accessibility audits, motion/reduced-motion accessibility, version-gated coding standards, multi-layout shells, theming, shared UI libraries, JSON-schema-driven dynamic reactive forms, permissions-ONLY route guards/UI gating (no role checks), authentication (OIDC/PKCE, token storage, auth interceptor, silent renewal — distinct from permission-based route guarding), routing architecture (resolvers, route-data conventions, lazy-loading boundaries), server-side rendering/hydration, runtime environment configuration for containerized SPAs, ongoing ESLint/Prettier CI governance, Storybook/visual-regression testing for shared component libraries, testing conventions (Testing Library, harnesses, e2e), i18n (shared key space with dotnet-localization, locale formatting, RTL), global error handling (ProblemDetails-aware), PWA/offline support, frontend telemetry (Application Insights JS SDK, trace-ID correlation), Nx/module-federation monorepo governance, third-party script governance (SRI, tag allow-list), OpenAPI/NSwag-generated API client governance (no hand-typed DTO drift), a shared UI kit for dialogs/toasts/confirmation prompts, frontend feature-flag governance coordinated with dotnet-feature-flags, and classic NgRx Store/Effects governance alongside signal-based state. Ships a reviewer, implementor, and support agent trio: @angular-reviewer finds violations, @angular-implementor fixes them, @angular-support diagnoses frontend runtime issues to root cause
